history.text: Give me a report on renewable energy trends

Global renewable energy adoption is accelerating rapidly, driven by falling costs for solar and wind power which have now made them the cheapest source of new electricity in most regions. Investment in these sectors is hitting record highs, particularly in Asia and Europe, while governments worldwide are tightening climate targets that mandate higher shares of clean energy in national grids. Technological advancements are improving battery storage efficiency and grid flexibility, helping to address intermittency issues. Hydrogen is emerging as a key solution for hard-to-decarbonize industries like shipping and heavy manufacturing. Despite these gains, challenges remain, including the need for massive grid upgrades, supply chain constraints for critical minerals, and balancing economic priorities with climate goals in developing economies.
